items.title: A server-side model call
items.scenario: Your web app must call an LLM without exposing the API key to browsers.
items.explanation: A Node backend reads the key from process.env and makes the call, so the secret never reaches the client.

items.title: A CLI ingestion tool
items.scenario: You want a command that embeds a folder of docs.
items.explanation: A Node script reads process.argv for the path and uses fs/path to walk and read files.

items.title: Streaming endpoint
items.scenario: You stream tokens from the model to the browser as they arrive.
items.explanation: Node's event loop handles the open connection while piping streamed chunks without blocking other requests.

items.title: Using a provider SDK
items.scenario: You'd rather use an official client than hand-roll fetch calls.
items.explanation: npm install the provider SDK and import it; package.json pins the version.
